_notes: Each entry is one destination/stop. recommended_nights is a default suggestion, not fixed. sub_itinerary is optional - use it when a stop is really a base for a multi-day excursion (e.g. Cusco -> Salkantay Trek) so the trek can be shown distinctly from town time. vibe_scores are 0-10, used for matching searches like 'ski trip' or 'romantic'. wandrly_notes are short first-person tips - shown in a distinct UI box. tom_verdict is one or two punchy sentences in your voice.

destinations.name: Andorra la Vella
destinations.summary: Surprisingly pretty mountain micro-state, cheap booze, not much to do

activities.name: Mountain scenery
activities.desc: Both the bus ride in and the city itself are really pretty, a pleasant surprise

activities.name: Stock up on cheap alcohol
activities.desc: Alcohol is so damn cheap, the main reason people stop here

activities.name: Lunch and a wander
activities.desc: Come in, take photos, get lunch, then move on

food_recommendations: Grab lunch in town, it's pleasant enough
nightlife: Quiet, not really a nightlife stop
transport_tips: Not very cheap bus tickets, comes in by road only, no airport or train
warnings: The thing is, there's just not much to do here beyond the scenery and shopping
similar_to: A pretty Pyrenees pit-stop between Spain and France
wandrly_notes: Honestly surprised me, super pretty everywhere, both the bus ride in and the city.
Alcohol is so damn cheap. The thing is there's just not much to do, come in, stock up, get lunch, leave.
destinations.tom_verdict: A pretty Pyrenees surprise but thin on things to do. Stock up on cheap grog, take some pics, move on.

destinations.name: Cairns & Tropical North
destinations.summary: Gateway to the Reef and the Daintree, tropical far north Queensland

activities.name: Great Barrier Reef
activities.desc: Dive or snorkel the reef, the main event up here

activities.name: Daintree Rainforest
activities.desc: Ancient rainforest meeting the sea, north towards Cape Tribulation

activities.name: Cooktown & the north
activities.desc: The rugged historic far north, a proper adventure drive

food_recommendations: Cairns esplanade has the bulk of the dining, fresh seafood throughout
nightlife: Cairns has a lively backpacker scene
transport_tips: A car opens up the Daintree, Mossman and Cooktown, the region is spread out
warnings: Stingers (marine jellyfish) in summer, swim in netted areas or stinger suits
It's the wet tropics, expect humidity and rain
similar_to: A tropical reef-and-rainforest base in Australia's far north
wandrly_notes: Part of the North Queensland run (Mossman, Cairns, Cooktown), the reef and the rainforest are the headline acts.
destinations.tom_verdict: Australia's tropical north: the Reef, the Daintree and rugged Cooktown. A car helps, mind the summer stingers.

destinations.name: Darwin & Kakadu
destinations.summary: Top End wetlands, ancient rock art and crocs

activities.name: Kakadu National Park
activities.desc: Vast wetlands, waterfalls and some of the world's oldest rock art

activities.name: Crocodile cruises
activities.desc: Spot saltwater crocs on the Top End's rivers and billabongs

activities.name: Darwin markets
activities.desc: Mindil Beach sunset markets are the social heart of the dry season

food_recommendations: Darwin's Mindil Beach markets for a multicultural feast at sunset
nightlife: Darwin has a relaxed tropical-city scene
transport_tips: A 4WD opens up more of Kakadu, especially in and after the wet
warnings: Crocodiles are a genuine danger, only swim where it's signed safe
Brutal heat and humidity, the dry season (May-Oct) is the time to go
similar_to: A wild, ancient Top End counterpoint to the coastal cities
wandrly_notes: The Kakadu and Darwin trip, the Top End wetlands, rock art and the croc country really stand out.
destinations.tom_verdict: The wild Top End: Kakadu's wetlands and rock art, croc cruises, Darwin's markets. Go in the dry, respect the crocs.

destinations.name: Kangaroo Island
destinations.summary: Wildlife-packed island off South Australia, beaches and bush

activities.name: Flinders Chase National Park
activities.desc: Remarkable Rocks and Admirals Arch, plus sea lions and wildlife

activities.name: Seal Bay
activities.desc: Walk among a wild Australian sea lion colony on the beach

activities.name: Wildlife everywhere
activities.desc: Kangaroos, koalas, echidnas and birds across the island

food_recommendations: Local honey, seafood and produce, the island is known for its food
nightlife: Very quiet, it's a nature escape
transport_tips: A car is essential, take the ferry from Cape Jervis or fly from Adelaide
warnings: Sights are spread out with long drives between them, watch for wildlife on the roads
similar_to: A wildlife-first island escape near Adelaide
wandrly_notes: One of the South Australia trips, wall-to-wall wildlife and dramatic coast, a lovely island escape.
destinations.tom_verdict: A wildlife-packed island near Adelaide. Sea lions at Seal Bay, Remarkable Rocks, roos everywhere. You need a car.

destinations.name: Phillip Island & Wilsons Prom
destinations.summary: Penguins, surf and the dramatic coast of southern Victoria

activities.name: Penguin Parade
activities.desc: The famous nightly march of little penguins up the beach

activities.name: Wilsons Promontory
activities.desc: Stunning granite peaks and beaches, some of Victoria's best hiking

activities.name: Bright & the high country
activities.desc: A pretty alpine town worth combining for the broader Victoria loop

food_recommendations: Coastal cafes and pubs, fish and chips on the island
nightlife: Quiet, this is nature and family territory
transport_tips: A car is needed, it's an easy drive south and east from Melbourne
warnings: The Prom's best walks are long, start early
Southern coast weather is changeable, layer up
similar_to: A wildlife-and-coast escape from Melbourne
wandrly_notes: The Victoria loop, Phillip Island penguins, Wilsons Prom hiking and Bright, a lovely mix of coast and high country.
destinations.tom_verdict: Penguins, granite peaks and coast south of Melbourne. The Prom's hiking is the highlight, pair it with Bright.

destinations.name: Innsbruck
destinations.summary: Alpine city with a spectacular mountain backdrop

activities.name: Nordkette cable car
activities.desc: Straight from the city up into the Alps for an unreal view

activities.name: Old town & Golden Roof
activities.desc: The pretty medieval centre beneath the peaks

activities.name: Mountain access
activities.desc: Hiking in summer, skiing in winter, right on the city's doorstep

food_recommendations: Standard Austrian fare, hearty but expensive
nightlife: A bit dead, especially in summer, don't come for the nights out
transport_tips: Compact and walkable, the mountains are a cable-car ride from the centre
warnings: Expensive as anywhere in the Alps
Felt a bit grimy and quiet in summer, probably better in ski season
Hostels here were dodgy
similar_to: An alpine Austrian city with the best mountain view on this list
wandrly_notes: A cool city with the most spectacular view of all time, the mountains right there.
Nightlife is a bit dead and it's expensive as hell, probably better not in summer, felt a bit grimy.
Super touristy but they were all nice, hostels were dodgy though.
destinations.tom_verdict: Comes for the jaw-dropping mountain view, not the nightlife. Take the cable car up, but maybe skip summer.

destinations.name: Vienna
destinations.summary: Palaces and architecture everywhere, some of the best in Europe

activities.name: Palaces and architecture
activities.desc: Probably one of the best in Europe, look up a list and pick a few

activities.name: Walk the sights
activities.desc: You can walk between the cool things and catch the occasional metro

activities.name: McFit gym
activities.desc: A great gym with a 7 euro trial pass, good to get a workout in

food_recommendations: Cheap Asian places to eat all around
We ate traditional Austrian deep fried chicken liver
nightlife: Meant to be ok per the hostel folks but no Budapest, don't prioritise it
transport_tips: Public transport is a little exy, not many ticket checkers so do as you will
Stay near a metro line to make life easy
warnings: After big nights in Zagreb and Budapest this is a slow-it-down city
similar_to: Budapest (grander but less wild)
wandrly_notes: Took it slow here after the nights out in Zagreb and Budapest, no big nights out.
Palaces and architecture everywhere is probably one of the best in Europe, just pick some to visit.
McFit was a great gym with a 7 euro trial pass.
destinations.tom_verdict: A grand, palace-filled slow-down city. Pick a few palaces, stay near a metro, save the big nights for Budapest.

destinations.name: Bruges
destinations.summary: Tiny, absolutely beautiful, so much fun

activities.name: Walk the canals
activities.desc: Best off walking around and finding stuff yourself, it's beautiful

activities.name: Belgian beer
activities.desc: Unholy good, all of it super delicious, TripAdvisor has decent recommendations

activities.name: Waffles and food
activities.desc: Waffles, pizza, burgers, a bit of searching needed but delicious

food_recommendations: Not very cheap but delicious, around 8-10 euro a meal, walk and find your own spots
nightlife: Super fun nightlife for such a small place, lean into it
transport_tips: Tiny and walkable, no transport really needed
warnings: Food isn't cheap, budget 8-10 euro per meal
similar_to: A smaller, prettier, beer-soaked Amsterdam
wandrly_notes: I adore this city, super small, absolutely beautiful, so so so much fun.
2 nights is plenty, the nightlife is super fun then have some chilled out days.
The beer is unholy good, god damn, all of it is super delicious.
destinations.tom_verdict: Tiny, beautiful and unexpectedly fun. Two nights, do a hostel not a hotel, drink the beer.

destinations.name: Caye Caulker
destinations.summary: No cars, just golf carts and Caribbean calm

activities.name: Snorkel or dive the reef
activities.desc: The barrier reef is right there, trips run constantly

activities.name: The Split
activities.desc: A swim spot and bar where the island splits, sunset beers are the move

activities.name: Just relax
activities.desc: Walkable, friendly and unhurried, this is a place to slow right down

food_recommendations: Paradiso cafe on the north side of south is great, balanced on price
nightlife: Relaxed, the Split bar is the social hub
transport_tips: No cars or bikes, just golf carts, the whole island is super walkable
Currency is fixed to USD at 1:2, just bring USD and use it the whole time
warnings: Not a cheap quick getaway, it's marketed for relaxing not budget
Ferry queues are huge, get there early for tickets and chill for the day
similar_to: Bocas del Toro (both Caribbean islands, Caye Caulker smaller and calmer)
wandrly_notes: On arrival seems very relaxed and friendly, already offered weed five-plus times in an hour.
No cars or bikes, just golf carts, the whole island is super walkable, very Caribbean.
Currency is fixed to USD at 1:2 so just bring USD and use it the whole time, everyone takes it.
destinations.tom_verdict: Golf-cart island time on the reef. Bring USD, snorkel, drink at the Split, and get to the ferry early.

destinations.name: Siem Reap
destinations.summary: Gateway to the vast Angkor temple complex

activities.name: Angkor Wat sunrise
activities.desc: The famous dawn view over the world's largest religious monument, go early to beat the crowds

activities.name: Angkor temple circuit
activities.desc: Ta Prohm's jungle-swallowed ruins and the Bayon faces are the other must-sees, hire a tuk-tuk for the day

activities.name: Pub Street
activities.desc: The lively nightlife strip in town, cheap drinks and a good backpacker buzz

food_recommendations: Cheap and tasty Khmer food all over town
Old Market area for local eats
nightlife: Pub Street is the centre of it, cheap and busy
transport_tips: Hire a tuk-tuk driver for a full day around the temples, it's the easy way to do the circuit
Buy a multi-day Angkor pass if you want more than one temple day
warnings: Temples get very hot and crowded midday, start at sunrise
Dress respectfully at the temples, shoulders and knees covered
similar_to: A Cambodian counterpart to the ruins-and-jungle of Tikal
wandrly_notes: An older trip (2016) so the detail is a bit faded, but Angkor genuinely lived up to the hype.
The temples beyond Angkor Wat itself, especially the jungle-covered ones, were the real highlight.
destinations.tom_verdict: An older trip for me, but Angkor is unforgettable. Sunrise at the Wat, tuk-tuk the temple circuit, wind down on Pub Street.

destinations.name: La Fortuna

activities.name: Cerro Chato hike
activities.desc: The best hike here, no real track, scrambling, ropes, mud, swimming in the crater lake

activities.name: Dusk river rafting
activities.desc: A river raft at dusk to spot all sorts of wildlife along the banks

activities.name: Arenal volcano 1554 track
activities.desc: Fun hike with good views of the volcano

food_recommendations: Eat at local sodas like Visquez or Hormiga to save money
Hostels with kitchens are a good budget move
nightlife: Not really a nightlife scene here
transport_tips: Hiring a car is the easiest way around Costa Rica
Local buses work but involve transfers, about $4 USD per hour
warnings: CR buses are punctual, arrive 15 minutes early
Always carry cash in Costa Rica
similar_to: Monteverde for the nature, but La Fortuna has more activities
wandrly_notes: La Fortuna is quintessential Costa Rica. All the animals, activities and a great town.
Eat at sodas like Visquez or Hormiga to save money.
Cerro Chato is the best hike, no real track, lots of scrambling and mud. Bring clothes you don't mind getting dirty.
For wildlife and birds, definitely hire a guide.
3-4 nights for a budget backpacker, 4-5 for a more relaxed stay.
destinations.tom_verdict: If you're going to Costa Rica, La Fortuna is a must-do stop. Absolutely loved it here.

destinations.name: Liberia
destinations.summary: Northern gateway town for Rincon de la Vieja national park

activities.name: Rincon de la Vieja
activities.desc: Volcano walks, canyoning and a beautiful waterfall track, the reason to be here

activities.name: La Leona waterfall
activities.desc: The waterfall and canyoning route in the park is a highlight

activities.name: Cheap local Sodas
activities.desc: The town's small family diners do great, cheap Costa Rican plates

food_recommendations: Eat at the local Sodas, cheap and good casados
American chain stores around town if you need them

transport_tips: Hiring a car is the way to do Costa Rica, the shuttles here are dodgy
If busing, ask your hostel about the local system, around 4 USD an hour with transfers
CR buses leave sharp, arrive 15 minutes early with cash
warnings: Costa Rica's border guards require proof of onward travel, have a ticket ready
The town itself isn't much, it's all about the national park
similar_to: A national-park base town like a drier, northern La Fortuna

Rincon de la Vieja national park is the reason to be here, the volcano walks and waterfall track are fantastic.
It says not to swim at the waterfall but everyone does.

destinations.name: Manuel Antonio
destinations.summary: A small but wildlife-packed national park with beaches

activities.name: National park trails
activities.desc: All the tracks take 3-5 hours total, so much wildlife, none too hard

activities.name: Beach swim
activities.desc: Take a swim when done but watch out, monkeys try to steal your bags

activities.name: Quepos town
activities.desc: The cheaper nearby town, comida tipica is 24h with good tasty casado

food_recommendations: In Quepos, comida tipica is 24h and does good tasty casado
Eat a big breakfast before the park, no food inside
nightlife: Quiet, it's a national park town
transport_tips: Bus every 15 mins each way for 380 colones, bring small change
warnings: Watch out, monkeys will try to steal your bags on the beach
No food in the park, eat a big breakfast and go in early
similar_to: A more compact, beachy version of Costa Rica's bigger parks
wandrly_notes: Is it worth travelling all the way here? Depends how much you like wildlife, the park is amazing.
Eat a big breakfast then go in early, no food in the park, then leave hungry in early arvo.
Quepos is the actual town nearby and way cheaper, comida tipica is 24h and good.
destinations.tom_verdict: A small park bursting with wildlife. Big breakfast, go early, watch the bag-thief monkeys, base in cheaper Quepos.

destinations.name: Monteverde
destinations.summary: Cloud forest, night tours and incredible bird life

activities.name: Night tours
activities.desc: Genuinely fun, you see a different side of the forest

activities.name: Day forest tours
activities.desc: Cool and great for bird life, reserves often close 3-4pm to prep for night tours

activities.name: Wildlife around town
activities.desc: So much to see, book tours a few days ahead as they sell out

Monteverde Backpackers was fab, book tours a few days in advance as they sell out.
It can get really cold and really wet here even in dry season, prep accordingly.
destinations.tom_verdict: Cloud forest, brilliant birds and fun night tours. Give it 3-4 nights, book ahead, and pack for cold and wet.

destinations.name: San Jose
destinations.summary: The workaday capital and connecting hub, better than its reputation

activities.name: National Museum
activities.desc: Genuinely great on Costa Rican history and identity, worth the stop

activities.name: City centre & main street
activities.desc: A proper downtown to wander, plenty of food options

activities.name: Day trips
activities.desc: Lots of pricey but good day trips run from the city

wandrly_notes: Only here for an afternoon and a night but I didn't hate it, it's a big connecting city with a proper centre.
The national museum is seriously great for Costa Rican history and identity.
Didn't regret the stop, CR Backpackers was a great hostel if you know a bit of Spanish.
destinations.tom_verdict: A connecting-hub capital that's better than people say. Do the excellent national museum and don't feel bad about one night.

destinations.name: Zagreb
destinations.summary: Crazy cheap, beautiful, a real highlight

activities.name: Museum of Broken Relationships
activities.desc: Go here, find the scab, it's so worth it

activities.name: North vs south of the station
activities.desc: North is classical euro with eastern flair, south is Soviet-style blocks, cool how different they are

activities.name: Bar crawl
activities.desc: A lovely bar crawl and an incredible amount of tequila

transport_tips: Don't trust the bus or train timetable, our bus left 35 minutes early for no reason

Don't trust the timetables, things leave early
similar_to: Budapest (cheaper and smaller, similar wild fun)
wandrly_notes: Huge fan of this city, fucking amazing and crazy cheap everything.
Museum of Broken Relationships, go here, find the scab, it's so worth it.
Nightlife is very fun but there's honestly not that much to do, fun for 2 nights max then move on.

destinations.name: Prague
destinations.summary: Beautiful old town, incredibly cheap beers, great hostel scene

activities.name: Petrin Hill and tower
activities.desc: Climb the mini Eiffel Tower for great city views, pay to go up

activities.name: Prague Castle and cathedral
activities.desc: The castle complex is huge and worth half a day, cathedral is impressive

activities.name: Old Town wander

Petrin Hill with the Eiffel Tower and the castle/cathedral are both must-dos.
Budget hostel was a fantastic experience and in a great spot, could walk everywhere.
Trams are easy to use and get you to most places.

destinations.name: El Tunco
destinations.summary: Cheap, surf-focused beach town, just relax

activities.name: Surf the beach
activities.desc: Super cheap and surf-focused, the beach is just perfect for it

activities.name: Do nothing

activities.name: Dive shop
activities.desc: There's a dive shop if you want a break from surfing

nightlife: Chilled surf-town nightlife
transport_tips: Well connected to San Salvador by chicken bus, or further afield by shuttle, Uber is super cheap

similar_to: A cheaper, surfier El Salvador answer to Sri Lanka's Hiriketiya
wandrly_notes: Hard to write about because I was only here one night, top of my mind is damn I wish I stayed longer.
Stayed in Hammock Plantation a bit west of the main area and it was amazing.
Everything is super cheap, uses USD and Uber is so cheap, spend 2-3 nights.

destinations.name: Helsinki
destinations.summary: Not a huge amount to do in the city, but a good launch pad north

activities.name: Irish pub at night
activities.desc: The place to be at night, unless you like techno

activities.name: Wander the centre
activities.desc: A pleasant enough city centre to explore for a day

activities.name: Head north (if time)

Drinks are super expensive so pre-drink heaps, the Irish pub is the place to be, free entry.
Honestly not much else to do here, Finland is beautiful if you go north into the bush.

destinations.name: Paris
destinations.summary: Beautiful but expensive, the Eiffel Tower slaps

activities.name: Eiffel Tower at sunset
activities.desc: A bottle of wine watching sunset on the Eiffel Tower is marvellous

activities.name: Musee d'Orsay
activities.desc: Museums are a better option than galleries in my opinion, at least you can say you went

So expensive, budget accordingly

Lots of scammers and dodgy vendors everywhere, keep your wits about you.

destinations.name: Kazbegi (Stepantsminda)

activities.name: Gergeti Trinity Church
activities.desc: A scenic 2-3 hour hike up to a church with Mount Kazbek as the backdrop

activities.name: Truso Valley walk

destinations.name: Berlin

activities.name: Museum Island

destinations.name: Athens
destinations.summary: A genuinely awesome city, beautiful and easy to love

activities.name: The Acropolis
activities.desc: Iconic, but very limited water and shade on the walk up, be prepared

activities.name: Pubs and locals
activities.desc: Pubs are very very fun and all the Greeks are super friendly

activities.name: Just wander
activities.desc: Doesn't need much info, it's just a really cool city to explore

food_recommendations: Bakeries and groceries are ridiculously cheap, can be a bit dodgy on freshness
nightlife: Very fun pubs, stay in a hostel because it's that kind of city, Pagration area
transport_tips: Make sure your accommodation is near the centre, buses are dodgy
Trams are quite good and the metro is alright, both recommended
warnings: Limited water and shade walking up the Acropolis, bring both
Beaches have nice water but it's dead flat and way worse than Sydney
similar_to: A friendlier, cheaper, more ancient Rome
wandrly_notes: Very very awesome city, looks beautiful, everything is awesome, it doesn't need much info.
Highly recommend staying in a hostel here because it's that kind of city, Pagration!
Pubs are very fun and all the Greeks are super friendly.
destinations.tom_verdict: A genuinely awesome, friendly, cheap city. Stay in a hostel, do the Acropolis early with water, hit the pubs.

destinations.name: Antigua
destinations.summary: Beautiful colonial town, base camp for the Acatenango volcano trek

activities.name: Acatenango volcano trek
activities.desc: A tough 2-day hike with lava views from Fuego, the reason to come here

activities.name: Central market
activities.desc: Amazing to get lost in, cheap food court at the back left if you can find it

activities.name: Fuego side hike
activities.desc: Extra tough add-on from Acatenango but worth every step for the lava and explosions

food_recommendations: Central market food court has lunch for about $25 quetzales if you speak Spanish
nightlife: Small town, limited but friendly
transport_tips: Buy a shuttle from Guatemala City airport, about $20 USD
Tropicana is the best agency for Acatenango
warnings: Acatenango summit gets to -10C with wind chill, bring all warm gear including scarf, hat and beanie
Spend a full day acclimatising in Antigua before the trek to avoid altitude sickness
similar_to: Huaraz for the trek-base-town vibe

sub_itinerary.name: Acatenango and Fuego volcano trek
sub_itinerary.summary: A 2-day, 30km trek including the optional but incredible Fuego side hike to see lava and explosions up close

days.title: Antigua to Acatenango base camp
days.desc: 11km hike to camp, arrive for sunset views of Fuego erupting

days.title: Summit and Fuego side hike
days.desc: 4am summit for sunrise, then optional 4km each way to Fuego for lava views, return to Antigua

wandrly_notes: Acatenango is the real reason to be here, a tough 2-day hike but just do all of it including Fuego.
Tropicana is the best agency for the trek.
Bring all your warm gear, it gets to -10C with wind chill at the summit.
The central market is amazing, cheap food court at the back left if you can find it.
Spend a full day in Antigua first to adjust to altitude before the trek.
destinations.tom_verdict: The Fuego hike is the reason I would come back. Worth every cent and every bead of sweat.

destinations.name: Flores
destinations.summary: Lake island town, gateway to the great Tikal ruins

activities.name: Tikal at sunset
activities.desc: Pay extra for the sunset ticket, quiet park, animals out, a magic walk back in the dark

activities.name: Yaxha ruins
activities.desc: A quieter ruin site, great for a half day before or after Tikal

activities.name: Lake Peten Itza
activities.desc: Kayak or swim off the island town, food here is cheap and big

food_recommendations: Food is cheap, expect 55-65 Quetzal max for a decent big meal
Wild Bean or similar is a good restaurant with big portions
nightlife: Low-key, it's more a ruins base than a party town
transport_tips: Long but doable overnight shuttle or a short domestic flight from Guatemala City/Antigua
warnings: Tikal's sunset ticket is an orange foreigner wristband, they check it at the big temples so go up early
Festival in January has cheap food but constant explosions all night, bring earplugs, ends 15 Jan
similar_to: A jungle-and-lake base, like a Central American Siem Reap
wandrly_notes: Tikal, strongly recommend paying extra for the sunset ticket, but not on a Sunday when locals get free entry.
Food in Flores is cheap, expect 55-65 Quetzal max for a decent big meal.
Festival happens in January with super cheap market food but so many random explosions all night, bring earplugs.
destinations.tom_verdict: A lake-island base for the brilliant Tikal ruins. Pay for the sunset ticket (not Sundays), eat cheap, bring earplugs in Jan.

destinations.name: Budapest
destinations.summary: Stunning, cheap, incredible nightlife. One of Europe's best cities.

activities.name: Ruin bars and Instant Club
activities.desc: Ruin bar district is busy 7 nights a week in summer, a must-do nightlife experience

activities.name: River cruise
activities.desc: Cheap river cruise to see the Parliament and bridges lit up at night

activities.name: Margaret Island pools
activities.desc: Public pools on the island are fun and a fifth of the price of the famous thermal baths

food_recommendations: Everything is cheap, just wander and eat
nightlife: Ruin bars into Instant Club is the move, close to Wombat hostel which is excellent
Boat parties on the Danube are apparently great too
transport_tips: Ticket checkers are everywhere on public transport, always pay
warnings: Stay centrally near the ruin bars to make life easy
similar_to: Prague for the cheap nightlife, but Budapest has better scenery
wandrly_notes: Loved this city, it's stunning, cheap and so much fun. Could easily spend a week.
Stay centrally near the ruin bars to make life easy.
Ticket checkers are everywhere on public transport, you absolutely must pay.
A river cruise is cheap and the best way to see the main sights.
The pools on Margaret Island are just as fun as the famous baths at a fifth of the price.
destinations.tom_verdict: One of my favourite cities in the world. Go back for a week at some point, this place is just amazing.

destinations.name: Canggu
destinations.summary: Gym, massage, beach and chill, not a culture stop

activities.name: Beach and cafe days
activities.desc: A relax-and-recharge spot, walking distance to the beach is ideal

activities.name: Gym and wellness
activities.desc: Day passes to gyms like Pucuk are great for around 50k

activities.name: Nightlife
activities.desc: Use Google for what's on each day, the scene moves around

food_recommendations: Crate Cafe is great with huge portions for around 70k
Manca 2 restaurant about 50-60k for a decent sized meal
nightlife: Use Google for events and nightlife, it changes by the day
transport_tips: A pain to get from the airport, a grab is around 300k and can take 2-3 hours, don't get ripped off
No need for a car rental, millions of grab bikes and cars around
warnings: Very easy to pay for something overpriced, don't get ripped off on massages or grabs
similar_to: Hiriketiya (both relaxed surf-and-wellness towns)
wandrly_notes: Not a place for culture or history, it's where you relax, hit the gym, get a massage and chill.
A pain to get from the airport, a grab is 300k, don't get ripped off, can take 2-3 hours.
Good massages, definitely recommend, but it's very easy to pay for something overpriced.
destinations.tom_verdict: A gym-massage-beach-chill town, not a culture hit. Stay walking distance to the beach and don't get grab-scammed.

destinations.name: Jakarta
destinations.summary: A bit of a hole but oddly likeable, cheap and real

activities.name: Walk the local areas
activities.desc: A crazy maze of shops and alleys, just cool to walk through

activities.name: National Monument
activities.desc: The monument is cool, museums are meant to be ok

activities.name: Indonesian McDonald's
activities.desc: Cheap and very odd, worth a visit for the novelty

food_recommendations: Indonesian McDonald's is cheap and odd, grab is 1/5 the price of Sydney for getting around
nightlife: Blok M is where the super rich Indonesians go, per a local friend
transport_tips: Driving is faster than the train, grab is here and as good as ever, about 1/5 of Sydney prices
warnings: The pollution is insane, a thick smog that partially blocks the sun sometimes
Not sure how safe the local maze areas are but I was fine
similar_to: A grittier, smoggier mega-city stopover
wandrly_notes: Everyone told me this city was a hole and they're kinda right, but I didn't hate it.
Coolest thing I did was walk through the local areas, a crazy maze of shops and alleys.
The pollution is insane, you can see a thick smog that partially blocks the sun.
destinations.tom_verdict: A smoggy mega-city most people skip, but walking the local alleys is oddly great. Cheap, real, two nights tops.

destinations.name: Uluwatu
destinations.summary: Classic Bali, cliffs, temples and diving

activities.name: Diving
activities.desc: All the boats go to the same spots so just go by price, SEA was fantastic

activities.name: GWK statue & Barong
activities.desc: The big statue and Barong dance were a genuine surprise highlight

activities.name: Uluwatu Temple
activities.desc: You're there for the monkeys and the clifftop setting more than the temple

food_recommendations: Easy to get ripped off, explore around and use the internet
nightlife: Lots of variation, explore around, easy to get ripped off
transport_tips: Grab bikes are about 3 dollars for 30 mins, BYO helmet, Gojek can be cheaper
I stayed inland and it was fine
warnings: The temple is meh, you go for the monkeys
Diving is cool but very crowded, wouldn't plan a Bali trip around it alone
similar_to: Canggu (Uluwatu is cheaper and more classic Bali)
wandrly_notes: Uluwatu feels like classic Bali, a lot cheaper than Canggu and has all the classic things.
Diving, all the boats go to the same spots so just go by price, SEA was fantastic.
Went to the big GWK statue and Barong which was actually really great, definitely recommended.
destinations.tom_verdict: Classic Bali on the cliffs, cheaper than Canggu. Dive by price, do the GWK statue, the temple's really about the monkeys.

destinations.name: Yogyakarta
destinations.summary: Real Indonesia, temples, caves and cool history

activities.name: Borobudur & Prambanan
activities.desc: Both world-class temples, doable in one big but exy day, sunrise is from a nearby hill not the temple

activities.name: Jomblang cave
activities.desc: Seriously cool with a famous light beam, an unmissable adventure

activities.name: Tilldrop nightlife
activities.desc: Live music every night, a great mix of locals and tourists

food_recommendations: Get a huge meal plus drink for 40k at a Warung buffet, don't get ripped off
A nice, not stupidly overpriced pizza place near Mediterranean (which is overpriced)
nightlife: Tilldrop and surrounds is peak, live music every night, heaps of locals and tourists
transport_tips: Train in and out is good, just buy your ticket in advance, they sell out all the time
If a group, a grab car from the airport is easier but probably slower
warnings: Wonderloft hostel has a whole wall of activities because there's so much to do
similar_to: A cultural, temple-rich counterpoint to beachy Bali
wandrly_notes: I liked it here, felt a lot more like real Indonesia with some cool history.
Highlights are Borobudur and Prambanan, the sunrise is from a nearby hill not the temple itself.
Jomblong caves was also seriously cool, and you can get a huge meal plus drink for 40k at a Warung buffet.
destinations.tom_verdict: The real, cultural Indonesia. Do Borobudur and Prambanan, the Jomblang cave is unmissable, eat at Warung buffets.

destinations.name: Rome
destinations.summary: Stunning at night, ancient everywhere, great cheap pizza

activities.name: Colosseum & Forum
activities.desc: Super easy to buy tickets in person on the day, go morning-ish for a mid-arvo slot

activities.name: Trevi Fountain at night
activities.desc: All the sights are stunning at night, the fountain especially

activities.name: Pantheon area
activities.desc: A great cheap pizza and mozzarella shop sits near the Pantheon

food_recommendations: Pizza and mozzarella are very cheap and very good near the Pantheon
Don't pay more than 10 euro for a pizza anywhere, ours were all 8-10 and great
nightlife: Rome works very late, everything open and busy till at least 11 in summer
transport_tips: The full experience Colosseum+Forum ticket is worth it, around 22 euro each
Buses are regular but never the right one, often faster to catch one going roughly the right way and hop off
warnings: From 2023 they're rolling out card tapping but it was barely there, bring cash
Smells a lot like cigarettes everywhere, like much of France and Italy
similar_to: Athens (Rome is grander and pricier, Athens friendlier)
wandrly_notes: Super super easy to buy Colosseum and Forum tickets in person on the day, go morning-ish.
All the Rome sights are stunning at night, especially the fountain.
Don't pay more than 10 euro for a pizza anywhere, ours were all 8-10 and very good.
destinations.tom_verdict: Ancient, stunning, and open late. Buy Colosseum tickets in person on the day, never pay over 10 euro for pizza.

destinations.name: Kyoto
destinations.summary: Quiet, atmospheric former capital, can get very cold in winter

activities.name: Fushimi Inari
activities.desc: Thousands of orange torii gates climbing the hillside, go early to avoid crowds

activities.name: TeamLab digital art
activities.desc: Surprisingly good immersive digital art installation

activities.name: Themed bars wander
activities.desc: Small, often quirky themed bars scattered through the city, including a rock bar

food_recommendations: A great Mexican restaurant near Rancho shrine, a fun surprise in Kyoto
nightlife: Small and quiet compared to Osaka, but some great themed bars
transport_tips: Can be done as a day trip from Osaka if short on time, but an overnight is worth it
warnings: Most paid temples can be seen via a side path without paying, not always worth the entry fee
Gets very cold in winter
similar_to: Nara for a smaller, quieter version
wandrly_notes: Much quieter than Osaka or Tokyo, and can get very cold in winter.
Fushimi Inari is genuinely worth it.
Most paid temples can be seen via a side path without paying, not always worth the entry fee.
Can be done as a day trip from Osaka if short on time, but still worth an overnight.
destinations.tom_verdict: The calm counterpoint to Osaka and Tokyo, go in spring or autumn if you can.

destinations.name: Matsumoto
destinations.summary: Gorgeous castle town, a great one-night stopover

activities.name: Matsumoto Castle
activities.desc: Gorgeous, especially with the winter/NY light show, going inside is worth it

activities.name: City Museum
activities.desc: Surprisingly good, worth it if you need to kill time

activities.name: Coffee Abe
activities.desc: Great and cheap breakfast

food_recommendations: Coffee Abe for cheap breakfast
Station food options are bad but there's bento inside the gate itself
nightlife: Quiet, it's a one-night castle-town stop
transport_tips: Well worth a stopover between other Japan stops
warnings: The castle floor is cold inside, but going in is still worth it
similar_to: A smaller castle-town cousin of Japan's bigger cities
wandrly_notes: Stayed here for one night and loved it, Matsumoto Castle is gorgeous, especially with the winter light show.
City museum was actually surprisingly good and worth checking out to kill time.
The station had bad food options but has bento inside the gate itself.
destinations.tom_verdict: A gorgeous castle town and a perfect one-night stop. See the castle (and its winter light show), eat at Coffee Abe.

destinations.name: Nagano
destinations.summary: Proper winter town with beginner-friendly skiing and great onsens

activities.name: Ryuoo Ski Park
activities.desc: Very beginner-friendly skiing with cheap gear hire, take the long train then free shuttle

activities.name: Mamejima Yutaruan onsen
activities.desc: Authentic outdoor onsen, a perfect way to finish a ski day

activities.name: Zenko-ji Temple
activities.desc: Huge, actively-used temple that gets a lot of snow in winter

transport_tips: Public transport gets expensive here, a car makes life much easier if staying a few days
warnings: The Jigokudani snow monkey park is a bit of a tourist trap and somewhat unethical, take it or leave it
similar_to: Niseko but smaller and cheaper
wandrly_notes: Proper winter town, the skiing at Ryuoo ski park is very beginner-friendly with cheap gear hire.
Finish a ski day at Mamejima Yutaruan for an outdoor onsen, genuinely amazing.
Public transport gets expensive here, a car makes life much easier if staying a few days.
destinations.tom_verdict: Great value introduction to skiing in Japan, the onsen after is half the appeal.

destinations.name: Osaka
destinations.summary: Much livelier than Kyoto, great food and nightlife

activities.name: Dotonbori at night
activities.desc: Iconic neon canal-side strip, walk it with a drink in hand

activities.name: Osaka Aquarium
activities.desc: A genuine highlight, including their coral regeneration programs

activities.name: Shinsekai
activities.desc: Slightly daggy retro shopping and food district, fun to explore

food_recommendations: Street food throughout Dotonbori, takoyaki and okonomiyaki are local specialties
nightlife: Dotonbori and Namba have the best concentration of bars and izakaya
transport_tips: Stay near Tennoji or Namba for easy transport connections
similar_to: Tokyo but smaller, friendlier, and more food-focused
wandrly_notes: Much livelier than Kyoto, Dotonbori at night with a drink in hand is great.
The aquarium is a genuine highlight, including their coral regeneration programs.
Stay near Tennoji or Namba for easy transport connections.
destinations.tom_verdict: The food alone justifies a stop here, pair it with Kyoto for a great contrast.

destinations.name: Tokyo
destinations.summary: Massive, endlessly interesting city, impossible to see it all so don't try

activities.name: Shibuya and Shimokitazawa
activities.desc: Stay near here for easy access to the centre, great for wandering alleys and shops

activities.name: Imperial Palace grounds
activities.desc: A calmer, more historic counterpoint to the neon districts

activities.name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building
activities.desc: Free observation deck with great skyline views

food_recommendations: Convenience stores (konbini) are genuinely excellent and open 24/7
nightlife: Shibuya and Shinjuku for variety, smaller izakaya bars for something quieter
transport_tips: Load Suica onto your phone for transport and shops, but note any balance is void once you leave Japan
Metro lines close around midnight and taxis are expensive, plan your last train
warnings: Some famous spots like Meiji Shrine and Jimbocho are often a bit underwhelming despite reputation
similar_to: Seoul for a similarly huge, neon-lit Asian capital
wandrly_notes: 4 to 5 nights is a good amount of time given the size of the city.
Staying near Shibuya, within about 15 minutes by train of the centre, works really well.
Get Suica loaded on your phone for transport and convenience stores, but note any balance is void once you leave Japan.
Metro lines close around midnight and taxis are expensive, plan your last train carefully.
destinations.tom_verdict: You cannot 'do' Tokyo, just pick a neighbourhood to base in and let yourself wander.

destinations.name: Riga
destinations.summary: Pretty old town, fun but a little quiet

activities.name: Old town
activities.desc: Quite pretty, recommend staying near the station if only here a night or two

activities.name: Aussie Pub
activities.desc: Fun and Australian of course

activities.name: Wander the centre
activities.desc: A nice place if a little quiet, easy to stroll

food_recommendations: Plenty around the old town
nightlife: Fun if a little quiet, the Aussie Pub is a laugh
transport_tips: Stay near the station if only here a night or two
warnings: Some hostels have no laundry, check before booking
similar_to: Kaunas (similar Baltic charm, Riga a touch bigger)
wandrly_notes: Not here very long but seemed like a fun place if a little quiet.
The old town is quite pretty, recommend staying near the station for a short stay.
Aussie Pub is fun and Australian of course.
destinations.tom_verdict: A pretty, quiet Baltic stop. Stay near the station, the old town is lovely, the Aussie Pub is a laugh.

destinations.name: Kaunas
destinations.summary: Surprisingly lovely, small, quiet and really pretty

activities.name: Old town
activities.desc: Some nice restaurants, chilled out and lovely

activities.name: Castle and the point
activities.desc: The castle and area by the point near the old town is beautiful

activities.name: St Michael's & town church
activities.desc: Worth visiting, all these churches are in use, don't walk into a wedding like I did

food_recommendations: Heaps of good cheap food, 10 euro for dinner goes a lot further than Western Europe
Restaurants close earlier than everywhere else, especially midweek
nightlife: Quiet, this is a chilled-out small town
transport_tips: Apple Maps does NOT work here, use Google Maps for public transport
If you have coins, easiest way around is buying tickets on the bus, arrive and leave by bus
warnings: Restaurants close earlier than everywhere, especially midweek
similar_to: Riga (Kaunas is smaller, quieter, even more underrated)
wandrly_notes: Genuinely surprising how much I loved this city, super small, quiet but really pretty.
Important, Apple Maps does not work here, use Google Maps instead for public transport.
Both locals we met had never met an Australian before, and most speak excellent English.
destinations.tom_verdict: A tiny, quiet surprise of a city. Use Google Maps not Apple, eat cheap, the castle and old town are lovely.

destinations.name: Kota Kinabalu
destinations.summary: Borneo base for Mount Kinabalu, islands and rainforest

activities.name: Mount Kinabalu
activities.desc: Borneo's towering peak, a serious two-day climb for the fit and the reason many come

activities.name: Island snorkelling
activities.desc: The Tunku Abdul Rahman marine park islands are a short boat ride offshore

activities.name: Sunset at the waterfront
activities.desc: The KK waterfront has one of the best sunsets in Southeast Asia

food_recommendations: Fresh seafood at the night markets by the water
nightlife: Low-key waterfront bars
transport_tips: Book Mount Kinabalu climbs well ahead, permits are limited
Island boats leave from the city jetty
warnings: Climbing Kinabalu needs a permit and guide, and reasonable fitness
Older trip, so double-check current operators and prices
similar_to: A Borneo wilderness base, rainforest and reef in one
wandrly_notes: The 2012 Borneo leg, an old trip, but the sheer wildness of it, the mountain and the rainforest, left a mark.
destinations.tom_verdict: An old trip but Borneo is wild in the best way. Climb Kinabalu if you're fit, otherwise islands, reef and that waterfront sunset.

destinations.name: Kuala Lumpur
destinations.summary: Modern, multicultural capital of towers and street food

activities.name: Petronas Towers
activities.desc: The iconic twin towers, great by day and lit up at night

activities.name: Batu Caves
activities.desc: Colourful Hindu cave temple up a steep rainbow staircase, just north of the city

activities.name: Jalan Alor food street
activities.desc: The famous open-air hawker street, the place to eat at night

food_recommendations: Jalan Alor for the full hawker experience
The mix of Malay, Chinese and Indian food is the real draw
nightlife: Changkat Bukit Bintang is the main bar strip
transport_tips: The city monorail and trains are cheap and easy
Grab is widely used and affordable
warnings: Older trip so details are hazy, verify opening times before you go
similar_to: A cheaper, more multicultural regional-hub city like Singapore
wandrly_notes: From the 2012 Malaysia and Borneo trip, an old one, so the notes are faded, but the towers and the food scene stuck.
destinations.tom_verdict: An old trip for me, but KL's towers, Batu Caves and hawker food still stand out. A couple of nights does it.

destinations.name: Mauritius
destinations.summary: Indian Ocean island of lagoons, reefs and beaches

activities.name: Lagoon beaches
activities.desc: Turquoise lagoons and white sand ring the island, the main event

activities.name: Snorkelling & diving
activities.desc: Warm reef-protected water makes for easy, lovely snorkelling

activities.name: Black River Gorges
activities.desc: Green inland national park for a change from the coast

food_recommendations: Creole cooking with Indian, Chinese and French influences, really good
nightlife: Relaxed, mostly resort and beach-bar based
transport_tips: Hiring a car gives the most freedom to circle the island
warnings: A honeymoon-and-resort island more than a backpacker one, budget accordingly
similar_to: A calmer, more resort-focused Indian Ocean beach escape
wandrly_notes: From the 2019 trip, one I still need to write up properly, but the lagoons and the water were the standout.
destinations.tom_verdict: A trip I still owe proper notes on, but the lagoons and reefs are gorgeous. A relaxed, beachy island escape.

destinations.name: Bacalar
destinations.summary: The lagoon of seven colours, kayaking and calm

activities.name: Kayak the lagoon
activities.desc: Fantastic and cheaper than the overpriced boat tours, you can paddle to the boat parties for free

activities.name: Cenote Azul
activities.desc: A deep dark cenote right on the lagoon's edge

activities.name: Free swim spots
activities.desc: Free water access after Calle 14, decent if slightly busy, no need to pay for a club

food_recommendations: Cocina Orizaba does breakfast and lunch, massive portions at real Mexican prices
Around 110 pesos for infinite tortillas, a meal, rice and soup
nightlife: La Catrina for a salsa party is fantastic with a group, mix of locals and tourists
transport_tips: The ADO bus station is NOT where Apple Maps marks it, it's about 500m back towards Tulum near Calle 42
warnings: Anyone saying Bacalar is undiscovered by tourists is kidding themselves
Prices similar to Tulum if not more expensive, 2-3 nights max unless you love birds or lagoon activities
similar_to: Tulum (Bacalar is calmer and lagoon-focused)
wandrly_notes: Getting a kayak is fantastic to see the lake and cheaper than the overpriced water activities.
If you're game, kayak over to the boat parties across the lagoon and join in without much difficulty.
The ADO bus station is not where Apple Maps marks it, it's near Calle 42.
destinations.tom_verdict: A seven-colour lagoon made for kayaking. Paddle to the boat parties, eat at Cocina Orizaba, 2-3 nights is plenty.

destinations.name: Mexico City
destinations.summary: Huge, friendly and brilliant for food

activities.name: Anthropology Museum
activities.desc: Ridiculously worth it, one of the great museums of the world

activities.name: Teotihuacan
activities.desc: Worth it but a long day with sun and walking, the guides give really useful info

activities.name: Practise Spanish
activities.desc: Few people speak English and locals are super friendly even if your Spanish isn't perfect

food_recommendations: Street food everywhere, cash is a must for stalls and tickets
nightlife: Clubbing with the hostel is really fun
transport_tips: Metro covers integrated bus and train routes and is really good, some routes close Sundays for bike riding
Cash is needed for tickets and anything on the street, ATM fees are sad but necessary
warnings: Traffic is insane at peak times, be prepared
Staying in the city centre is a must
similar_to: A bigger, friendlier, more chaotic European capital with better tacos
wandrly_notes: Speaking a bit of Spanish and having cash are the two most important things here.
Come on a Sunday for free museums, the Anthropology museum is ridiculously worth it.
Most tourists are from Mexico or other Spanish-speaking countries and locals are super friendly, it's not France.
destinations.tom_verdict: Huge, friendly and a food paradise. Come Sunday for free museums, the Anthropology museum alone is worth the trip.

destinations.name: Tulum
destinations.summary: Ruins, cenotes and a stark wealth-gap beach

activities.name: Chichen Itza day tour
activities.desc: Find a shorter one, combine with a cenote and lunch, just go, it's so worth it

activities.name: Cenote swims
activities.desc: The Yucatan is full of them, the freshwater sinkholes are magic

activities.name: Beach access hack
activities.desc: Walk through La Eufemia taco shop for free beach access, the town itself is more chill

food_recommendations: La Eufemia taco shop, walk through for free beach access too
nightlife: Beach clubs are pricey, the town is more chill
transport_tips: Bus (ADO) from Cancun airport, cheaper than a shuttle but a bit dodgy
Show up at the Cancun airport bus stop 20 mins early, lots leave early
warnings: Super expensive on the beach side, the actual town is more chill
Worth walking to the beach side just to see the wealth gap
similar_to: Bacalar (Tulum busier and pricier, Bacalar calmer)
wandrly_notes: Chichen Itza day tour is so worth it, find a shorter one, please just go, it's so cool.
La Eufemia is a taco shop you can walk through to get free beach access.
Worth walking to the beach side to see the wealth gap, the beach itself is just ok.
destinations.tom_verdict: Ruins, cenotes and a wild wealth gap. Do a Chichen Itza tour, hit the cenotes, taco-shop your way onto the beach.

destinations.name: Tangier
destinations.summary: Modern Europe meets Morocco in the markets

activities.name: Medina markets
activities.desc: A maze of streets and stalls, dodgy-feeling but not unsafe, everything is cheap

activities.name: Cape Spartel & Hercules Cave
activities.desc: Grab a cab from the ferry terminal, the lighthouse is sick, the cave is meh

activities.name: American Legation
activities.desc: A cool little museum in the medina, worth an hour

food_recommendations: Street food in the medina is super cheap, restaurants targeting tourists run 8-14 euro
nightlife: Low-key, this is more a markets-and-medina city than a nightlife one
transport_tips: Fast ferry from Tarifa, about 1 hour, get your passport stamped on the ferry itself to skip arrival hassle
For Cape Spartel, 20 euros got me a 2.5 hour guided tour around the coast, they'll try for 50
warnings: You MUST bring cash, euros and USD accepted, bring small notes and coins as things are so cheap
Apparently has pickpockets in the medina though I didn't see any
similar_to: A North African echo of a Vietnamese market city
wandrly_notes: Really cool city, modern Europe meets Vietnam with the markets, everything is super cheap.
Bring small euro notes and coins, water was 0.40 euro and a pain au chocolat was 0.20.
On the ferry from Tarifa get your passport stamped on the boat itself to prevent arrival hassle.
destinations.tom_verdict: A cheap, chaotic, brilliant medina city and an easy ferry from Spain. Bring small cash, day-trip the lighthouse.

destinations.name: Amsterdam
destinations.summary: Awesome, friendly, super tourist-friendly and easy to get around

activities.name: Best public transport of the trip
activities.desc: The best public transport of the entire trip and not too expensive

activities.name: Red Light Secrets museum
activities.desc: So worth visiting, it answers all your questions and you meet the monsters

activities.name: Bars and randos
activities.desc: Bars are super fun here if you meet random people

food_recommendations: Food and drinks aren't cheap but it's a fun city to eat in
nightlife: Super fun, especially if you meet random people in the bars
transport_tips: The best public transport of the entire trip, use it freely
warnings: If taking edibles, check there's actually THC in them, some are THC-free
Hostels are 100x better than hotels here but expensive
similar_to: A bigger, busier Bruges
wandrly_notes: Another highlight, awesome city, super friendly, super fun, very tourist friendly and easy to get everywhere.
The Red Light Secrets museum is so worth visiting, it answers all your questions.
Combine an edible with the Body Worlds museum for the full tourist experience.
destinations.tom_verdict: Friendly, fun and the best transport of the trip. Do a hostel, hit the bars, the Red Light museum is worth it.

destinations.name: Christchurch
destinations.summary: Arrival city, rebuilt and arty

activities.name: Botanic Gardens
activities.desc: A relaxed first stop to stretch your legs after the flight

activities.name: Street art tour
activities.desc: Central Christchurch has excellent post-earthquake street art scattered through the CBD

food_recommendations: Riverside Market in the CBD for a good food hall fix
nightlife: Decent for a smaller city, concentrated in the CBD
transport_tips: Main South Island international airport, pick up a rental car here
destinations.tom_verdict: Not a destination in itself, but a perfectly good 1-2 night bookend for a South Island loop.

destinations.name: Lake Tekapo
destinations.summary: Turquoise lake beneath the Southern Alps

activities.name: Church of the Good Shepherd
activities.desc: Iconic small stone church right on the lakeshore, best at sunrise

activities.name: Stargazing
activities.desc: Part of a Dark Sky Reserve, book a stargazing tour if the weather is clear

food_recommendations: Limited options, the lakeside cafe near the church is the main one
transport_tips: About 3 hours drive from Christchurch through the Canterbury Plains
warnings: A short stop is enough, most people use this as a one-night scenic break
similar_to: Lake Louise (Canada)
destinations.tom_verdict: One night is right, see the church, do the stargazing if clear, then move on.

destinations.name: Queenstown
destinations.summary: Adventure capital, stunning but busy

activities.name: Ben Lomond via Tiki Trail
activities.desc: Free hiking route up the mountain with better views than the paid gondola

activities.name: Milford Sound day trip
activities.desc: A long day but the fiord scenery is genuinely unmatched, go early to beat tour buses

activities.name: Queenstown Gardens
activities.desc: A free, relaxed walk right by the lake if you need a lower-key day

food_recommendations: Fergburger is touristy but genuinely good, go early or late to skip the queue
nightlife: The best in the South Island, busy every night of the week in season
transport_tips: About 1 hour from Wanaka via the Crown Range, the highest sealed road in NZ
warnings: Prices spike heavily in peak season, book accommodation well ahead
similar_to: Interlaken (Switzerland) for the adventure-capital-on-a-lake vibe
wandrly_notes: Skip the paid gondola, the free Tiki Trail up Ben Lomond has better views for $0.
destinations.tom_verdict: Worth the hype for the activities, but base in Wanaka if budget or crowds are a concern.

destinations.name: Wanaka
destinations.summary: Lakeside town, less crowded than Queenstown

activities.name: Isthmus Peak hike
activities.desc: A quieter alternative to Roys Peak with equally stunning lake views

activities.name: Rob Roy Glacier track
activities.desc: A relatively easy walk to a hanging glacier and waterfalls

activities.name: That Wanaka Tree
activities.desc: The famous lone tree in the lake, best at sunrise to avoid crowds

food_recommendations: Lakefront cafes around the Wanaka Tree area
nightlife: A handful of good bars, calmer than Queenstown
transport_tips: About 3 hours from Lake Tekapo via Lindis Pass, one of the most scenic drives in the country
similar_to: Queenstown but quieter, a good alternative base
wandrly_notes: Skip the busy Roys Peak track and try Isthmus Peak instead, similar views, far fewer people.
destinations.tom_verdict: My pick over Queenstown for a base, same scenery, fraction of the crowds.

destinations.name: Leon
destinations.summary: Cool, safe, laid-back colonial city, volcano boarding base

activities.name: Volcano boarding
activities.desc: The must-do here, super fun, great views, sunset was magic at Cerro Negro

activities.name: Revolution Museum
activities.desc: Politically heavy but great and informative on Nicaragua's violent history, 3 USD

activities.name: 4pm walking tour
activities.desc: Great info and finishes on the must-do church roof for an amazing sunset

food_recommendations: Desayunazo for great cheap breakfast, or Manana Manana for upmarket
Central market for cheap lunch, street food for cheap good dinner
nightlife: Relatively laid-back but fun, the walking tour and a smoothie is a good combo
transport_tips: Stay in the centre, walking distance to the cathedral, connection to San Juan del Sur is easy
warnings: Connection to El Tunco is hell, but pick your poison through Honduras
similar_to: Antigua (both colonial bases, Leon is grittier with volcano boarding)
wandrly_notes: Overall a really cool city and felt very safe, stay in the centre near the cathedral.
The Revolution Museum is politically heavy but great for 3 USD, I can't recommend it enough.
The must-do is volcano boarding, super fun, great views, sunset was magic at Cerro Negro.
destinations.tom_verdict: A safe, cheap colonial base for volcano boarding. Do the Revolution museum and the rooftop-sunset walking tour.

destinations.name: San Juan del Sur
destinations.summary: Mostly an American surf-tourist town, casual but small

activities.name: Surf nearby beaches
activities.desc: Shuttle to Playa Hermosa or Playa Madera, chill spots with decent surf

activities.name: Sunday Funday
activities.desc: The famous pool crawl, very much the scene here

activities.name: Cheap eats
activities.desc: A 24h restaurant near Pachamama (Comedor) does 3 USD meals

food_recommendations: Comedor near Pachamama, 24h, meals for 3 USD
nightlife: Sunday Funday pool crawl is the big draw, otherwise casual
transport_tips: Quite connected, easy transport and close to the border
Shuttle to the surf beaches is 6-10 USD, bring food and water
warnings: The beach in town is dodgy, watch your stuff and honestly don't swim
Lots of tourist tax, you might be better off in Ometepe or Granada unless you surf
similar_to: El Tunco (both surf towns, SJDS more American and party-focused)
wandrly_notes: Mostly an American tourist town, cool and casual but very small, better off in Ometepe or Granada unless you surf.
Don't rent a surfboard till you arrive at the beach in case the waves are bad.
The beach in town is dodgy, watch your stuff and don't swim, there's a bike track to Playa Hermosa to skip the entry fee.
destinations.tom_verdict: A small American surf-party town. Good for Sunday Funday and nearby surf, but skip the dodgy town beach.

destinations.name: Oslo
destinations.summary: Absolutely stunning and developed, but it can be a real party pooper

activities.name: Fram Museum
activities.desc: Crazy worth visiting, as is the opera house for a view of the city

activities.name: Vigeland sculpture park
activities.desc: Really worth checking out, it's stunning

activities.name: Sunset at Ekebergparken
activities.desc: Statue park with a great sunset view over the city

food_recommendations: Grunerlokka has a row of lovely bars, basically like Newtown
nightlife: You basically can't have a casual drink anywhere except home or a 10 euro pub
transport_tips: Coming to/from the airport, catch the REGIONAL not express train, 3 mins slower and saves 7-8 euro
Public transport is stupid expensive at 3-4 euro per trip with lots of ticket checkers, hire a bike or walk
warnings: Public drinking in any sense is completely illegal, even on your own balcony
Alcohol can't be sold after 8pm, expect to pay 75-110 kroner per drink
Under 20 you can't drink over 22% or even enter venues serving it
similar_to: Stockholm (similarly pricey and pretty, Oslo stricter on alcohol)
wandrly_notes: Absolutely stunning and really developed, beautiful, but FUCK ME it can be a party pooper in every sense.
Catch the regional not express train from the airport, 3 minutes slower and saves 7-8 euro.
Norwegian locals are very fun, shoutout to Frida, Mina and Kristoffer.
destinations.tom_verdict: Stunning and developed but the alcohol laws are brutal. Do the Fram museum and Vigeland park, take the regional train.

destinations.name: Bocas del Toro
destinations.summary: Caribbean island with great diving, bioluminescent plankton and beaches

activities.name: Scuba diving with Pirates
activities.desc: Fantastic marine life and soft corals, bioluminescent plankton on night dives is incredible

activities.name: Beach hopping
activities.desc: Accessible by $2.50 bus or water taxi, several beautiful beaches to explore

activities.name: Thursday to Saturday pub crawl
activities.desc: The island comes alive for the weekend pub crawl

food_recommendations: Eat local for $5-7 per meal, much cheaper than tourist spots
nightlife: Packed Thursday through Saturday for the pub crawl, quieter rest of the week
transport_tips: Beaches accessible by Estrella bus for $2.50 or water taxi
warnings: If travelling solo, find a group for non-diving days, much more fun
similar_to: Caye Caulker for Caribbean island vibes but bigger and livelier
wandrly_notes: One of my favourite parts of the trip. Pirates diving is recommended, they give discounts on subsequent dives and free kayak rental.
The bioluminescent plankton on the night dive is ridiculously good.
The island is packed Thursday to Saturday for the pub crawl then much quieter.
If travelling solo, find a group when not diving, it's way more fun.
Eat local for $5-7 per meal.
destinations.tom_verdict: Definitely one of my favourite parts of the whole Central America trip. The night diving here is unreal.

destinations.name: Panama City
destinations.summary: The most developed city in Central America, a great connection hub

activities.name: The Causeway
activities.desc: Check out the causeway for a stroll and views

activities.name: Canal Museum
activities.desc: A good museum, worth a visit

activities.name: Cerro Ancon
activities.desc: Climb it for a view over the city, the old town is also worth it

food_recommendations: Expensive unless you go to the dodgier, poorer parts near Cerro Ancon
nightlife: Decent, it's a big metropolitan city
transport_tips: Very central, fly basically anywhere in EU, US, SA, or boat to Colombia
warnings: Expensive unless you seek out the cheaper, poorer parts
Hearing lots of cartel money is here
similar_to: A glossier, more developed Central American capital
wandrly_notes: Very big and metropolitan, above and beyond the most developed in all of Central America.
A cool city for 2 days, then use its fantastic connections to continue your trip.
You won't regret a few days here but I wouldn't make it a priority, spectacular city overall though.
destinations.tom_verdict: The slick, developed hub of Central America. Two days for the canal, old town and causeway, then fly onward.

destinations.name: Cusco
destinations.summary: Gateway to the Sacred Valley, most visitors use this as a base for the Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu

activities.name: San Pedro Market
activities.desc: Huge local market, great for breakfast and people-watching before acclimatising

activities.name: Plaza de Armas & Cathedral
activities.desc: The historic centre, best visited in your first day or two while adjusting to altitude

activities.name: Sacsayhuamán
activities.desc: Massive Inca stonework ruins a short walk above the city, good easy-altitude activity

food_recommendations: San Pedro Market for cheap, authentic breakfast
nightlife: A lively backpacker bar scene around the Plaza de Armas
transport_tips: Most international connections route through Lima first, domestic flight or bus to Cusco
warnings: Altitude sickness is common, spend 1-2 days acclimatising in Cusco before starting any trek
similar_to: Kathmandu as a trek gateway city

sub_itinerary.name: Salkantay Trek to Machu Picchu
sub_itinerary.summary: A 5-day trek from Cusco's Sacred Valley over the Salkantay Pass (4,650m) to Machu Picchu, the most popular alternative to the Inca Trail

days.title: Cusco to Soraypampa
days.desc: Drive from Cusco, then trek to the base camp beneath Salkantay mountain

days.title: Salkantay Pass to Chaullay
days.desc: The toughest day, cross the 4,650m pass, then descend into cloud forest

days.title: Chaullay to La Playa / Santa Teresa
days.desc: Continue descending through coffee and fruit plantations

days.title: Hidroeléctrica to Aguas Calientes
days.desc: Walk the train tracks to the town beneath Machu Picchu

days.title: Machu Picchu
days.desc: Early entry to Machu Picchu itself, then train/bus back to Cusco

destinations.tom_verdict: If you're booking 'Cusco', know that most of those nights will actually be spent on the trek, plan the city time around acclimatisation, not sightseeing.

destinations.name: Warsaw
destinations.summary: Beautiful old town and the best skyline we'd seen so far

activities.name: Warsaw Museum in the old town
activities.desc: Absolutely huge, answers all your questions, bottom floors are the most interesting

activities.name: Palace of Culture and Science
activities.desc: Really worth seeing, a beautiful building, the city has a great skyline

activities.name: Old town wander
activities.desc: Beautiful, buy your transport tickets, it might be cheaper to buy a bundle than 24h pass

food_recommendations: Grocery stores are unholy cheap, though a lot is a bit dodgy
nightlife: Didn't see much, came down with a virus here
transport_tips: Buy tickets, possibly cheaper to get an x-amount bundle than a 24 hour pass
warnings: Most cheap supermarket things are a bit dodgy, check freshness
similar_to: Wroclaw (Warsaw is bigger with a better skyline)
wandrly_notes: Old town is beautiful, the Warsaw Museum there is a must, it's huge and answers all your questions.
Going into the city to see the Palace of Culture and Science is really worth it, a beautiful building.
Warsaw probably had the best skyline of any city we'd been to so far.
destinations.tom_verdict: Beautiful old town, brilliant museum, best skyline of the trip. Do the Warsaw Museum's lower floors.

destinations.name: Wroclaw
destinations.summary: Stunning old town in the middle, very Polish on the outskirts

activities.name: Cathedral Island
activities.desc: Go up the top of St John the Baptist, about 3 AUD and super worth it

activities.name: Traditional Polish restaurants
activities.desc: Heaps around the square, super tasty and not too exy

activities.name: Club square
activities.desc: A square near the main square filled with clubs, walk into whichever takes your fancy

food_recommendations: Konspira restaurant nearby is really awesome
Keep meals under about 35 zloty or you're being ripped off
nightlife: Clubs still open late on a Sunday, a whole square of them near the main square
transport_tips: Walkable in the old town
warnings: Everything in the main square and old town has a crazy tourist tax
Pubs can be way too exy or cheap, we paid 7 zloty a beer at one lovely pub (3 zloty = 1 AUD)
similar_to: Prague (cheaper, less touristy, similar old-town beauty)
wandrly_notes: Stunning old town in the middle but very Polish on the outskirts.
Go up the top of St John the Baptist on Cathedral Island, it's like 3 AUD so super worth it.
Try the traditional Polish restaurants around the square, keep anything under 35 zloty or you're being ripped off.
destinations.tom_verdict: A gorgeous old town wrapped in real Poland. Climb the cathedral tower, eat Polish, keep meals under 35 zloty.

destinations.name: Lisbon
destinations.summary: Hilly, sunny capital with great food, trams and an easy pace

activities.name: Alfama wander
activities.desc: Get lost in the oldest district, narrow lanes, miradouros (viewpoints) and fado bars

activities.name: Belem
activities.desc: Custard tarts at the original Pasteis de Belem, plus the Jeronimos Monastery and tower

activities.name: Jardim do Rio at sunset
activities.desc: Riverside park with food trucks and drinks, a great way to end the day

food_recommendations: Pasteis de Belem for the original custard tart
Bakeries inside supermarkets sell tarts for 0.40-0.70 euro and sandwiches for around 2.50 euro
nightlife: Lisboa Rio is touristy but fun and not too expensive by European standards
Bairro Alto for bars that spill onto the street
transport_tips: Credit card works directly on public transport
Oriente station is a bit far from the tourist core but very close to the airport
warnings: The lift up to the castle viewpoint is touristy and slow, walk up instead for better views from Zambeze restaurant and beyond
similar_to: Porto for a smaller, similarly hilly alternative
wandrly_notes: Credit card works on public transport here, a nice change after much of Europe.
Skip the famous funicular, Zambeze restaurant up the hill has a better view, and keep climbing for even better.
Jardim do Rio at sunset with some drinks and friends is one of the best simple things to do here.
The airport is efficient, you don't need to arrive 3 hours early, but customs is after the food court so allow time.
destinations.tom_verdict: Easy, beautiful, and cheap by European standards. A great first or last stop on a Europe trip.

destinations.name: Sintra
destinations.summary: Fairytale palaces and castles in the hills above Lisbon, best as a day trip

activities.name: Pena Palace
activities.desc: The iconic colourful palace on the hilltop, very popular so go early

activities.name: Moorish Castle
activities.desc: Cheaper alternative to the palace with great views and ancient walls to walk

activities.name: Quinta da Regaleira
activities.desc: Mysterious gardens with a famous spiral well, a bit more off the main trail

transport_tips: Easy day trip by train from Lisbon's Rossio station, about 40 minutes
warnings: Lots of walking and uphill sections, wear good shoes
wandrly_notes: Sintra is well worth visiting, go to the castle over the palace if on a budget, both are worth it if not.
Lots of walking, plan for a full day.
destinations.tom_verdict: A perfect day trip from Lisbon, just don't try to do every site, pick two and enjoy the pace.

destinations.name: Singapore
destinations.summary: Modern and easy but a little boring, 1.5 days is plenty

activities.name: Marina Bay Sands skytower
activities.desc: Worth every cent, spend here rather than on the Gardens trees

activities.name: Gardens by the Bay
activities.desc: Cool surrounds but don't waste money going up the trees

activities.name: Satay Street / Lau Pa Sat
activities.desc: Picks up around 7pm and seriously worth a visit, though busy

food_recommendations: Lau Pa Sat and Satay Street, busy from 7pm and worth it
Chinatown nearby has other options
nightlife: Around Chinatown and Lau Pa Sat, decent but not a wild scene
transport_tips: Public transport is great and easy, uses your credit card, English is the first language
warnings: Most things are overpriced, similar to Sydney prices
Staying in Little India was very cheap and close to everything
similar_to: A cleaner, more corporate, slightly duller regional hub
wandrly_notes: Interesting and clearly trying hard to be modern and western, but a little boring, 1.5 days is all you need.
I spent money to go up the Marina Bay tower, worth every cent, don't waste money on the Gardens trees.
Staying in Little India was very cheap and close to everything, a good idea.
destinations.tom_verdict: Modern, easy and a bit boring. A day and a half does it, splurge on the skytower, sleep cheap in Little India.

destinations.name: Barcelona
destinations.summary: Beautiful, busy and a bit pricey, beach plus Gaudi

activities.name: Sagrada Familia
activities.desc: Attending at least one Gaudi thing is so worth it, this is the one

activities.name: Gothic Quarter
activities.desc: Really fun and worth strolling around, super safe in the city

activities.name: Montjuic cable car
activities.desc: Going up the hill is a must, catch the cable car down instead of paying for castle entry

food_recommendations: Avoid the obvious tourist traps on La Rambla, head into the Gothic Quarter
nightlife: Beach nightclubs are not worth the exorbitant fee, lots of alpha males on beard care
transport_tips: Public transport is really good, staying near a metro is great, I loved near Sagrada Familia
The coach terminal at Sants is NOT signposted, it is off to the side, not at the bus terminal
warnings: You will be ripped off everywhere, just accept it, drink prices in clubs can be insane
Around the beach and wharf can be a little dodgier at night
similar_to: Madrid (Barcelona is busier and beachier but less liveable)
wandrly_notes: Super beautiful and fun, but you will be ripped off everywhere, just accept it.
Going up Montjuic is a must but paying for castle entry is a complete waste, buy the cable car instead.
Yeah Hostel near Sagrada Familia was amazing and the whole area is really nice.
destinations.tom_verdict: Gorgeous and fun but expensive and scammy. Do a Gaudi site, skip the castle entry, take the cable car down.

destinations.name: Madrid
destinations.summary: Effortlessly pretty and very liveable

activities.name: Tapas on La Latina
activities.desc: The best tapas street in the city, graze your way through the bars

activities.name: Retiro Park
activities.desc: Get a rowboat out on the lake, the park is beautiful and central

activities.name: Royal Palace & Plaza Mayor
activities.desc: The palace, the big cathedral and the grand plaza are all worth a wander

food_recommendations: Tapas crawl along Calle de la Cava Baja in La Latina
Mercado de San Miguel for a graze
nightlife: Clubbing with the hostel crowd is genuinely fun here
Malasaña and La Latina for bar-hopping
transport_tips: Get a monthly student transport card, it is legit 8 euros and unlimited
The C-x cercanias lines are separate to the metro with a separate Renfe card, allow time at the station
Chamartin station is a nightmare to navigate, you leave the station then re-enter for high speed trains
warnings: No public toilets anywhere in Spain, use fast food places whenever you can
similar_to: Lisbon (a touch grander and more landlocked)
wandrly_notes: Way more liveable and effortlessly pretty than Barcelona, I was a big fan.
Stay at Cats or near Atocha central station, it is the go for getting everywhere, trust me.
Day trip to Toledo is vital, Segovia is still cool and worth it but Toledo takes priority.
destinations.tom_verdict: Gorgeous, liveable and underrated next to Barcelona. Get the 8 euro student transport card and stay near Atocha.

destinations.name: Segovia
destinations.summary: Roman aqueduct and a fairytale cathedral, a quieter day trip

activities.name: The Aqueduct
activities.desc: The huge Roman aqueduct is super cool and worth the trip

activities.name: Cathedral
activities.desc: Super cool and worth it, the highlight of the town

activities.name: Park by the aqueduct
activities.desc: Cool view of the city and pretty chill, good to relax

food_recommendations: A little meh, standard Spanish tourist traps
nightlife: Quiet, it is a day trip town
transport_tips: Getting here is a shitshow by bus, pay the extra to just get the train all the way in
There is no ticket station for the bus at the train station, everyone buys on board
warnings: Definitely missing the charm of Toledo
Be careful walking into churches, lots have services on in the day
similar_to: Toledo (Segovia is the quieter, less charming sibling)
wandrly_notes: Getting here is a shitshow, pay the extra to just get the train all the way in, the 10 euros saved isn't worth it.
The cathedral and the aqueduct are worth the trip but it's definitely missing the charm of Toledo.
destinations.tom_verdict: Cool aqueduct and cathedral but it can't touch Toledo. If you only do one, do Toledo.

destinations.name: Seville
destinations.summary: The most atmospheric stop in Andalusia

activities.name: Real Alcazar
activities.desc: A near full-day, gorgeous Moorish palace, an absolute must visit

activities.name: Plaza de Espana
activities.desc: The grand half-moon plaza, one of the key sights in the city

activities.name: Cathedral & Giralda tower
activities.desc: Great view from the top but very crowded, the rest is a little meh

food_recommendations: Tapas in the Triana district across the river
nightlife: Heaps of fun, the riverside and Alameda areas are lively
transport_tips: You can finally tap your card to pay for the metro, Europe is in 2024
Reception everywhere is a bit ass so download maps offline
warnings: Alcazar gets busy, book ahead or arrive early
No public toilets, plan around fast food spots
similar_to: Granada (similar Moorish history, smaller)
wandrly_notes: Alcazar is a must, basically a full day and just gorgeous, the cathedral next door is less key.
You can see the whole city in a 2 night stay, 3 is more relaxed, tick off Alcazar, cathedral and Plaza de Espana.
OneFam Centro was a great hostel and nightlife was heaps of fun. If I come back I'll kayak the river.
destinations.tom_verdict: The most beautiful stop in Andalusia. Alcazar and Plaza de Espana are unmissable, do it in 2-3 nights.

destinations.name: Tarifa
destinations.summary: Windswept surf town at Europe's southern tip, gateway to Morocco

activities.name: Old town wander
activities.desc: Small but really pretty, stay near or east of the port where the action is

activities.name: Whale watching
activities.desc: The strait is one of the best places in Europe to spot whales and dolphins

activities.name: Kitesurfing beaches
