activities.desc: Huge windswept beaches, this is a kitesurfing capital for a reason

food_recommendations: Restaurants close 4-8pm then reopen till 11-12, plan around it
nightlife: Chilled surf town vibe, not a big nightlife scene
transport_tips: No train or airport, get the bus via Algeciras which has the train station
Ferry to Tangier is really easy, buses also run to Malaga or Seville 3-4 times a day
warnings: Restaurants all close 4-8pm, beware if you arrive hungry
Not many supermarkets, one Dia in town and one Lidl on the outskirts
similar_to: A windier, surfier Andalusian coast town
wandrly_notes: Huge regret is not having time to dive here, they have it and it looks fun, I'll be back.
Get a place near or east of the port, that's where all the action is and the city is super walkable.
Wouldn't come here for any reason other than diving or Morocco entry, but the ferry to Tangier is really easy.
destinations.tom_verdict: A surf-and-ferry town more than a destination. Come for the Tangier hop or the diving, stay near the port.

destinations.name: Toledo
destinations.summary: Stunning old hilltop city, a vital day trip from Madrid

activities.name: The Cathedral
activities.desc: Entry into the big church is worth it, the others are a bit meh

activities.name: Santa Cruz museum
activities.desc: Free and a highlight, just beautiful, a good spot to relax

activities.name: Viewpoints across the river
activities.desc: Walk the whole old town then cross for fantastic viewpoints worth some photos

food_recommendations: Standard but fine in the old town, it is a day trip so eat light
nightlife: Not a nightlife town, it is a day trip
transport_tips: About 20 euros all up for the train from Madrid central and incredibly worth it
Hidden free public toilets in the library by the army museum and in the Jewish quarter
warnings: Big day of walking with not much shade
Recommend 5-6 hours, 7 was a little too long
similar_to: Segovia (Toledo has way more charm)
wandrly_notes: So so gorgeous, about 20 euros for the train from Madrid central and incredibly worth it.
All the action is in the old town, across the river isn't much but a really cool view.
Recommend 5-6 hours here, I did 7 and it was a little too long of a day trip.
destinations.tom_verdict: The best day trip from Madrid, full stop. Beautiful old hilltop city, give it 5-6 hours.

destinations.name: Colombo
destinations.summary: Chaotic gateway city with great street food

activities.name: Galle Face Green
activities.desc: Sunset walk along the seafront esplanade, street food stalls come alive in the evening

activities.name: Pettah Market
activities.desc: Sensory overload market district, explore the spice and textile lanes

activities.name: Dutch Hospital precinct
activities.desc: Restored colonial quarter with good cafes if you need a calm reset

food_recommendations: Street food stalls along Galle Face Green at sunset
Pettah market for spices and snacks
nightlife: Quiet compared to the rest of the trip, mostly hotel bars
transport_tips: Fly into Bandaranaike Airport (CMB), about 40 minutes by tuk-tuk to the centre
warnings: Traffic can make short trips take longer than expected
similar_to: Bangkok (smaller, calmer version)
destinations.tom_verdict: Not a highlight, but a fine one-night landing pad before heading inland.

destinations.name: Ella
destinations.summary: Tea hills, hikes and the famous train line

activities.name: Nine Arches Bridge
activities.desc: Arrive around 8:15am to catch the train crossing for the classic photo

activities.name: Little Adam's Peak
activities.desc: Easy one-hour hike with sweeping views over the tea plantations

activities.name: Ella Rock
activities.desc: A tougher three-hour hike with far fewer tourists than Adam's Peak

food_recommendations: Cafes along the main strip do good Western/Sri Lankan fusion
nightlife: Small but social backpacker bar scene
transport_tips: The Kandy to Ella train is one of the great rail journeys, book well ahead, views are stunning from Nanu-Oya onward
similar_to: Pai (Thailand) for the laid-back hill-town backpacker vibe
wandrly_notes: Walk to Rawana Cave behind the falls, almost nobody goes there, it's a great quiet add-on.
destinations.tom_verdict: Easily worth 3 nights, the hiking, the train, and the chill cafe scene all genuinely deliver.

destinations.name: Galle
destinations.summary: UNESCO Dutch fort town, the most atmospheric stop

activities.name: Galle Fort ramparts
activities.desc: Walk the sea walls at sunset for the best light and ocean views

activities.name: Fort cafes and galleries
activities.desc: Small lanes inside the fort have excellent independent cafes and shops

activities.name: Jungle Beach
activities.desc: A short tuk-tuk ride north to a hidden cove, good for snorkelling

food_recommendations: Coconut sambal and the 'street burger' (a KFC-style local favourite)
nightlife: Quiet, mostly cafe/bar culture inside the fort
transport_tips: Coastal train from Mirissa/Matara, the clifftop section is one of the best short rides on the island
warnings: Fort walk is hot in the middle of the day, go morning or sunset
similar_to: Cartagena (Colombia) for the colonial fort-town feel
wandrly_notes: Stayed just outside the fort for cheaper, larger accommodation, only a $5 tuk-tuk into the fort itself.
Diving at Hikkaduwa (20 mins away) is bare-bones but cheap, around 80 USD for 2 dives plus gear.
destinations.tom_verdict: The most atmospheric stop on the island. Worth the full walk around the fort walls even though it's hot.

destinations.name: Hiriketiya
destinations.summary: Exactly like a smaller, cheaper Canggu but in Sri Lanka

activities.name: Dickwella & Hiri beaches
activities.desc: Dickwella beach is great, Hiri main beach is cool with better surf

activities.name: Snorkelling at the point
activities.desc: Great along the east side of Dickwella at the point and turtle point, rent or BYO gear

activities.name: Suicide Club tea plantation
activities.desc: Awesome, cheap and fun on the way over, definitely recommend

food_recommendations: Mcool is the best dessert store and cheaper than the others, 600 rupees for a big ice cream
Avoid Garlic Cafe / Blue Beach chill out lounge, huge wait times
nightlife: Quiet-ish, Dots is meant to be good
transport_tips: Hiring a paddleboard for sunset on Dickwella is a highlight, around 3k
warnings: Watch out for aggressive stray dog packs
similar_to: Canggu (Hiriketiya is the smaller, cheaper Sri Lankan version)
wandrly_notes: Literally exactly like a smaller, cheaper Canggu but in Sri Lanka.
Suicide Club tea plantation on the way over was awesome and cheap and fun, definitely recommend.
Watch out for stray dog packs which can be aggro, and Mcool is the best dessert store.
destinations.tom_verdict: A smaller, cheaper Canggu in Sri Lanka. Surf Hiri, snorkel the point, sunset paddleboard on Dickwella, mind the dog packs.

destinations.name: Kandy
destinations.summary: Sacred hill city with cooler air

activities.name: Temple of the Tooth
activities.desc: Visit during the early morning puja ceremony for the most authentic experience

activities.name: Kandy Lake loop
activities.desc: Easy 45-minute walk around the lake and through the old town

activities.name: Peradeniya Botanical Gardens
activities.desc: 60 acres of colonial-era gardens including a striking bamboo cathedral

food_recommendations: Lakeside cafes around Kandy Lake
nightlife: Limited, this is a calmer, more spiritual stop
transport_tips: Scenic train from Habarana/Sigiriya area, book first class in advance if possible
similar_to: Luang Prabang for the spiritual hill-town feel
destinations.tom_verdict: A good change of pace after Sigiriya, cooler, calmer, and a nice cultural anchor.

destinations.name: Mirissa
destinations.summary: South coast beach town, whales and sunsets

activities.name: Whale watching
activities.desc: Boats leave early morning, blue and sperm whales possible December to April

activities.name: Secret Beach
activities.desc: A 20 minute walk east along the coast from the main beach, mostly locals here

activities.name: Beachside seafood
activities.desc: Grilled catch of the day at the beachfront restaurants, best at sunset

food_recommendations: Beachfront seafood grills, best at sunset
nightlife: A few beach bars, low-key
transport_tips: Bus from Ella, or tuk-tuk from Matara train station, roughly 3 hours
warnings: Whale watching only worthwhile Dec-April, check season before planning around it
similar_to: Canggu (Bali) but smaller and quieter
wandrly_notes: Secret Beach is a 20 minute walk east of the main beach, mostly locals, much nicer than the main strip.
destinations.tom_verdict: Good for slowing down after the hill country, the whale watching is genuinely worth the early start in season.

destinations.name: Sigiriya
destinations.summary: Ancient rock fortress and cave temples

activities.name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
activities.desc: Climb at sunrise before the tour groups arrive, frescoes and mirror wall are highlights

activities.name: Pidurangala Rock
activities.desc: Hike up for the best view of Sigiriya itself, with a fraction of the crowd

activities.name: Dambulla Cave Temple
activities.desc: 150 Buddha statues across 5 caves, the largest cave temple complex on the island

food_recommendations: Two-storey tourist restaurants in town are reliable and good value
nightlife: Very quiet, early nights here are normal
transport_tips: Train from Colombo to Habarana then a short tuk-tuk, or a direct bus (about 4 hours)
warnings: Elephants do roam near farmland at night, listen for alarms/shooting sounds, this is normal elephant deterrence
similar_to: Bagan (Myanmar) for the ancient-monument vibe
wandrly_notes: Sigiriya is basically a must-do, climb in the morning pre-heat and pre-crowds, arrive by 7am at the latest.
Pidurangala is a scramble to get up but gorgeous views and gives the best photo of Sigiriya itself.
Stop in Dambulla on the way for the temples and the central fruit market, both surprisingly cool.
destinations.tom_verdict: One of the best mornings of the whole trip. Don't skip Pidurangala just because Sigiriya is the famous one.

destinations.name: Unawatuna
destinations.summary: Beautiful small tourist town, cheaper than Galle and Hiriketiya

activities.name: Main beach
activities.desc: Ok and pretty, no surf, but a lovely spot for a beer on the sand

activities.name: Weligama surfing
activities.desc: Went on the way in, was awesome

activities.name: Chameera Bike Tours
activities.desc: Amazing, got to ask heaps of questions and see lots, well worth it

food_recommendations: Plenty of cheap beachside spots
nightlife: Low-key beach-town nightlife
transport_tips: If getting hassled for beach entry prices, sneak in or go via the Tartarus Hotel alleyway for free
warnings: Diving is just ok and not too great, but it's not expensive
similar_to: Galle (Unawatuna is the cheaper, beachier neighbour)
wandrly_notes: Beautiful small tourist town, cheaper than Galle and Hiriketiya.
Chameera Bike Tours was amazing, got to ask heaps of questions and see lots, well worth it.
Great spot to finish the trip, enjoy a beer on the beach and chill.
destinations.tom_verdict: A cheap, pretty beach town to end a Sri Lanka trip. Do a Chameera bike tour, surf Weligama, beer on the sand.

destinations.name: Stockholm
destinations.summary: Beautiful, easy to get around, exy but super cool

activities.name: Museum island (Djurgarden)
activities.desc: Stunning, worth strolling, the Vasa and the wreck museum are both great

activities.name: Old town (Gamla Stan)
activities.desc: Very worth it, stay near central and walk to it

activities.name: Archipelago day trip
activities.desc: We went to the islands, so so beautiful, the ferry ride is the best part

food_recommendations: Restaurants are so exy, 7-Eleven or Coop is a genuinely good cheap option
See TripAdvisor for proper food
nightlife: Older crowd, nightlife aimed at 21-23+, dodged a bullet coming midweek
transport_tips: Zimmermann station area has the best free view of the city, go at night
Calisthenics parks everywhere if you want a workout
warnings: Buy alcohol in the day, shops close at night and you can't buy at the supermarket, must be 20
Even in summer it's cold, bring trackies and a light jumper regardless of season
similar_to: Oslo (both pricey Nordic capitals, Stockholm has more to do)
wandrly_notes: Beautiful city overall, exy but super cool, super easy to get around and worth coming back.
The whole museum island is stunning, the wreck museum is cool from a marine archaeology view, I loved it.
Sigtuna is meant to be cool but we went to the islands which was so so beautiful, the ferry ride was the best part.
destinations.tom_verdict: Beautiful, easy and expensive. Do museum island and the archipelago, buy booze in the day, pack warm even in summer.

destinations.name: Lucerne
destinations.summary: Storybook lake town ringed by spectacular mountains

activities.name: Lake Lucerne
activities.desc: The lake and mountain scenery is the whole point, just stunning, take a boat out

activities.name: Chapel Bridge & old town
activities.desc: The covered wooden bridge and the medieval centre are gorgeous

activities.name: Mountain excursions
activities.desc: Pilatus or Rigi for cable-car views over the lake and greenery

food_recommendations: Swiss cheese and beers are excellent but eye-wateringly expensive
Expect to pay a lot everywhere, self-catering saves money
nightlife: Quiet, this is a scenery-and-relaxation town
transport_tips: Swiss trains are famously punctual and scenic but pricey
The nearby Zug and Cham area is just as beautiful and worth the detour
warnings: Stupidly expensive everywhere, like 20 euros for a simple meal
Budget hard or self-cater to survive Switzerland
similar_to: A quintessential Swiss lake-and-mountain postcard
wandrly_notes: Stayed with friends around Zug and Lucerne and the scenery is just spectacular, mountains and greenery over the lake.
Crazy stunning, absolutely adored it, got fed far too many beers and cheese.
Stupidly expensive everywhere though, like 20 euros for a Bahn, tasty but a bit wack.
destinations.tom_verdict: Absurdly beautiful and absurdly expensive. The lake and mountains around Lucerne and Zug are worth every painful franc.

destinations.name: Zermatt
destinations.summary: Car-free alpine village beneath the Matterhorn

activities.name: The Matterhorn
activities.desc: The iconic pyramid peak looms over everything, the reason you come

activities.name: Gornergrat railway
activities.desc: A cogwheel train up to jaw-dropping glacier and peak views

activities.name: Skiing or hiking
activities.desc: World-class slopes in winter, spectacular trails in summer

food_recommendations: Mountain-hut fondue with a view is the classic splurge
nightlife: Some apres-ski bars, but it's mostly a mountain village
transport_tips: Zermatt is car-free, you arrive by train from Tasch
Everything runs on scenic mountain railways and lifts
warnings: One of the priciest spots in an already pricey country
Weather can hide the Matterhorn for days, allow buffer time
similar_to: A car-free, higher-altitude sibling to Switzerland's other alpine resorts
wandrly_notes: Part of the 2013 Switzerland trip, an older one, but the Matterhorn and the sheer scale of the Alps here are unreal.
destinations.tom_verdict: An older trip but the Matterhorn delivers. Car-free, gorgeous, brutal on the wallet, take the train up for the views.

destinations.name: Taipei
destinations.summary: Fascinating mix of western and Chinese culture with great day trips

activities.name: Jing Mei White Terror Museum
activities.desc: Phenomenal museum on Taiwan's dark pre-democracy history, a must-do

activities.name: Jiufen day trip
activities.desc: Must-do day trip, climb Teapot and Keelung mountains, stay for the old street at night

activities.name: Yangmingshan and Beitou walks
activities.desc: Beautiful rugged walks away from the city crowds

food_recommendations: Night markets everywhere, cash is king and saves money
nightlife: Maji Square is awesome, can bring your own drinks, mix of locals and tourists
transport_tips: Buy an EasyCard from a stand at the airport with cash, don't bother with the app
Can only top up with cash
warnings: The central station is huge and confusing when changing trains
similar_to: Seoul for the mix of modern and traditional
wandrly_notes: Buy an EasyCard from a stand at the airport with cash, don't bother with the app.
Jing Mei White Terror Museum is phenomenal, a must-do on Taiwan's dark pre-democracy history.
Jiufen is a must-do day trip, climb Teapot and Keelung Mountains if you have walking gear, both are steep.
Yangmingshan and Beitou are great for beautiful, rugged walks.
Maji Square at night is awesome for drinks, can BYO and it's a great mix of locals and tourists.
destinations.tom_verdict: A very interesting city with great day trips. Spent 6 days here and could have done more.

destinations.name: Bangkok
destinations.summary: Chaotic, fun capital with incredible food and temples

activities.name: Grand Palace and Wat Pho
activities.desc: Grand Palace is worth the cost, Wat Pho next door is also great

activities.name: Khao San Road at night
activities.desc: The centre of backpacker nightlife, quiet at 7:30 then picks up all night

activities.name: River cruise
activities.desc: A river cruise is really worth it, negotiate hard on price

food_recommendations: Yodpiman flower markets area is great for food
Siam Museum has a great show on level 3
nightlife: Khao San Road is the hub, hostel would be fun here
transport_tips: Grab or taxi from the airport is easiest, splitting a ride is cheap
Never pay more than 40 baht for a short tuk-tuk ride
warnings: Cash is king in Thailand
Once you've seen about 3 wats they all start looking the same
similar_to: Ho Chi Minh City for the chaotic energy
wandrly_notes: Stay near Khao San Road, this is the centre of nightlife. Super quiet at 7:30 then picks up all night.
Never pay more than 40 baht total for a short tuk-tuk ride.
Grand Palace is worth the cost, and Wat Pho is cool too.
A river cruise is really worth doing, negotiate hard on price.
Cash is king everywhere in Thailand.
destinations.tom_verdict: A cool big city worth a couple of nights. Don't try to see everything, just soak it in.

destinations.name: Chiang Mai
destinations.summary: Mountain city with temples, markets, elephants and incredible food

activities.name: Sunday night markets
activities.desc: Absolutely packed and super cheap, the markets within the wats are a highlight

activities.name: Ethical elephant sanctuary
activities.desc: A must-do, make sure it's ethical. GetYourGuide has good options

activities.name: Doi Suthep temple
activities.desc: Mountaintop temple with sweeping views, the best of the temples here

food_recommendations: Warorot markets for super cheap food and $4 fake designer goods
Western cafes available when you need a break from curry
nightlife: Smaller than Bangkok but still fun
transport_tips: Easy to get around, stay near old town for walking distance to most things
warnings: Sunday market crowds are insane, go early or late to avoid the worst of it
similar_to: Luang Prabang for the temple-city-in-the-mountains vibe
wandrly_notes: Warorot markets are super cheap, this is where you get your $4 fake designer stuff.
Sunday night markets are insane, packed and super cheap. The markets within the wats are the highlight.
Elephant sanctuary is a must-do, make sure it's ethical.
Doi Suthep, jungle temple and the tunnels one are all cool, in that order.
destinations.tom_verdict: A really cool city. The markets alone are worth the visit, and the elephant sanctuary is unforgettable.

destinations.name: Ko Samui
destinations.summary: A cool, chilled-out island with marine parks and beaches

activities.name: Ang Thong Marine Park
activities.desc: A must, do it with a relaxed day tour and allocate a full day

activities.name: Fisherman's Village markets
activities.desc: Worth it, with beach access on the pier at the right entry point

activities.name: Big Buddha
activities.desc: Free and cool enough

food_recommendations: Try the food and have fun, Chamon has the cheapest paddleboards and kayaks
nightlife: Chaweng is the big touristy nightlife town, Chamon is chill and relaxed
transport_tips: Grab is the Uber here and pretty cheap, renting a car gives good freedom
warnings: Chaweng is cheap and dodgy, Lamai can't decide what it is and ends up the worst
Ang Thong is on the far end of the island from Chaweng and the airport, which are both north
similar_to: A more developed, marine-park version of a Thai island escape
wandrly_notes: Overall a really cool and chilled out island, Ang Thong marine park is a must, allocate a full day.
Chaweng is the big touristy town, cheap and dodgy, Chamon is chill and relaxed with lovely vibes.
Fisherman's Village markets are worth it, there's beach access on the pier at the right entry point.
destinations.tom_verdict: A chilled island with a must-do marine park. Stay in mellow Chamon over dodgy Chaweng, give Ang Thong a full day.

destinations.name: Dili
destinations.summary: Off the beaten track diving paradise, Atauro Island is unmissable

activities.name: Atauro Island diving
activities.desc: The best diving I have ever done, a must-do at all costs

activities.name: Christo Rei viewpoint
activities.desc: Fantastic view, bring swimmers for the beaches at the base

activities.name: Dive Timor Lorosae
activities.desc: Great dive resort to stay at, you meet people and the vibes are excellent

food_recommendations: Castaways is a western restaurant near the dive resort, more expensive but good for culture shock recovery
nightlife: Very limited, this is a dive and chill destination
transport_tips: Need $30 USD cash for visa on arrival, prepare customs form QR code in advance
Microlet 12 is the local minibus, 25 cents a ride, tap the bar to get off
warnings: No reliable ATM at the airport, bring USD cash
About a day's worth of non-diving activities in the city
similar_to: Komodo for remote diving
wandrly_notes: Come prepared with the customs form QR code and $30 USD cash for the visa.
If you're here, go diving, it's not a question. Atauro Island is the best diving I have ever done.
Staying at a dive resort is the move, you meet people and the vibes are great.
Christo Rei is worth the visit, bring swimmers for the beaches at the base.
Catch microlet 12 around town, 25 cents a ride.
destinations.tom_verdict: Atauro Island diving alone makes this trip worth it. One of the most off-the-beaten-track destinations I've been to.

destinations.name: London
destinations.summary: Sydney-friendly, all in English, easy to get around once you crack the Tube

activities.name: Explore by Tube
activities.desc: Use Google Maps before entering, there are a lot of lines and it's confusing

activities.name: Leicester Square nightlife
activities.desc: Fun but super expensive, pre-drinks needed, and bloody hot inside

activities.name: Uni bars
activities.desc: Way cheaper, really social and fun, find a uni bar

food_recommendations: Endless options, but uni bars are the value play for drinks
nightlife: Leicester Square is fun but exy, pre-drink first, uni bars are cheaper and more social
transport_tips: Use Google Maps before entering the Tube to know exactly where to go
The Gatwick bus is way cheaper but slower and less comfy than the express train
Check your Heathrow terminal carefully, and watch night bus timetables
warnings: Drinks can be pricey, uni bars are the value option
Be careful with bus timetables in mornings and at night
similar_to: A bigger, pricier, English-speaking European capital
wandrly_notes: Very Sydney-friendly, all in English obviously and you can use a debit card.
Uni bars are way cheaper and really social and fun, we were at Court.
Hammersmith St Chris hostel was good for getting to the airport any time but a bit uncomfortable.
destinations.tom_verdict: Easy, English and expensive. Crack the Tube with Google Maps, drink at uni bars, take the Gatwick bus to save.

destinations.name: Long Beach
destinations.summary: Suburban, beautiful, great food, a more relaxed side of LA

activities.name: Marina and 2nd Street
activities.desc: Lots of action down by the marina and on 2nd Street

activities.name: Day trip into LA
activities.desc: Worth it for the spectacle, hop on a tourist bus to see the sights

activities.name: Panama Joe's
activities.desc: A lot of fun but a younger crowd

food_recommendations: Beautiful food and drinks, enjoy them, it's a cool place
nightlife: Panama Joe's is a lot of fun, younger crowd
transport_tips: The TAP system on bus/metro is too easy, just use that
Just fly into LAX, it's not that far, Long Beach is walkable-ish else Ubers and buses are super cheap
warnings: Best advice is to have a friend who lives here, else use a tourist bus to see LA
similar_to: A calmer, more suburban LA base
wandrly_notes: Best advice I can give is to have a friend that lives somewhere here, I was in Long Beach and loved it.
Beautiful, great food and drinks and more suburban, the TAP system on bus/metro is too easy.
Going into LA itself is worth it for the spectacle, but Long Beach is really cool.
destinations.tom_verdict: The relaxed, suburban side of LA. Hang by the marina and 2nd Street, day-trip into LA, ideally with a local friend.

destinations.name: Oahu
destinations.summary: Hawaiian beaches, volcanic ridges and surf

activities.name: Waikiki & North Shore
activities.desc: Classic Waikiki beach plus the legendary North Shore surf breaks

activities.name: Diamond Head
activities.desc: Hike the volcanic crater rim for a view over Honolulu

activities.name: Pearl Harbor
activities.desc: The historic harbour and memorial

food_recommendations: Poke bowls and plate lunches everywhere, food trucks on the North Shore
nightlife: Waikiki has the bulk of it, relaxed island pace
transport_tips: A car helps a lot to circle the island, though Waikiki itself is walkable
warnings: Expensive, it's a remote island, and the surf on the North Shore is no joke in winter
similar_to: A US-flavoured tropical island with serious surf
wandrly_notes: Part of the 2014 US trip, an early one, but the North Shore and the volcanic ridges left a real mark.
destinations.tom_verdict: An early trip but the surf and volcanic scenery hold up. Circle the island by car, mind the winter North Shore swell.

destinations.name: San Francisco
destinations.summary: Iconic hills, bridge and bay, a classic US west coast city

activities.name: Golden Gate Bridge
activities.desc: The iconic walk or cycle across the bridge and around the bay

activities.name: Alcatraz
activities.desc: The famous island prison tour out in the bay

activities.name: Cable cars and hills
activities.desc: Ride the cable cars up the famous steep streets

food_recommendations: Fisherman's Wharf for the classics, the Mission for great cheap eats
nightlife: Plenty across the city's neighbourhoods
transport_tips: Cable cars and the BART are the easy ways around, it's a walkable but very hilly city
warnings: Expensive, and it can be surprisingly cold and foggy even in summer
similar_to: A hillier, foggier, west-coast counterpart to other big US cities
wandrly_notes: An early trip from 2014 so the memories are a bit faded, but the bridge and the bay really stuck with me.
destinations.tom_verdict: An older trip for me but the hills, bridge and bay are unforgettable. Pricey and foggier than you'd expect.

destinations.name: Yellowstone
destinations.summary: Geysers, wildlife and vast wilderness, a bucket-list national park

activities.name: Old Faithful & geyser basins
activities.desc: The iconic geysers and surreal hot springs like Grand Prismatic

activities.name: Wildlife spotting
activities.desc: Bison, elk and bears across the valleys, especially at dawn and dusk

activities.name: Hiking and lookouts
activities.desc: Vast trails and viewpoints, the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone is a highlight

food_recommendations: Stock up outside the park, options inside are limited and pricey
nightlife: None, this is deep wilderness, that's the point
transport_tips: You need a car, the park is enormous, allow real driving time between sights
warnings: Keep a serious distance from wildlife, especially bison and bears
Weather changes fast at altitude
similar_to: A vast geothermal wilderness, unlike anywhere else
wandrly_notes: An early one (2014) so the notes are faded, but the geysers and the sheer scale of the wilderness stuck with me.
destinations.tom_verdict: A faded early trip but unforgettable: geysers, bison and endless wilderness. You need a car and a few days.

destinations.name: Danang
destinations.summary: Beach city between the mountains and nearby Hoi An

activities.name: My Khe Beach
activities.desc: A long stretch of city beach, easy and pleasant

activities.name: Hoi An day trip
activities.desc: The lantern-lit old town nearby is the real draw, gorgeous at night

activities.name: Marble Mountains
activities.desc: Cave temples and viewpoints in limestone hills just south of the city

food_recommendations: Central Vietnamese food is a highlight, try mi quang and banh xeo
nightlife: Low-key, the riverside and beach bars are the main scene
transport_tips: Easy to reach Hoi An by taxi or Grab for the evening lantern town
warnings: More of a base than a destination in itself, the nearby towns are the highlights
similar_to: A beachy base for the lantern town of Hoi An
wandrly_notes: From the 2016 trip, faded memories, but Hoi An nearby was the part everyone raves about for good reason.
destinations.tom_verdict: An older trip, mostly a base for gorgeous Hoi An. Beach by day, lantern-lit old town by night.

destinations.name: Hanoi
destinations.summary: Atmospheric old-quarter capital, gateway to Halong Bay

activities.name: Old Quarter wander
activities.desc: The tangle of narrow trading streets is the heart of the city, just get lost in it

activities.name: Halong Bay trip
activities.desc: The famous limestone-karst bay is a day or overnight trip out and a genuine highlight

activities.name: Hoan Kiem Lake
activities.desc: The calm central lake, lovely early morning when locals do tai chi

food_recommendations: Egg coffee is a Hanoi speciality, try it
Street pho and bun cha are the local staples
nightlife: Bia hoi corner in the Old Quarter for cheap fresh beer on tiny stools
transport_tips: The Old Quarter is walkable, which is the best way to feel the city
Book a reputable Halong Bay operator, quality varies a lot
warnings: Same scooter-heavy traffic as the south, cross with steady nerve
similar_to: A more historic, atmospheric northern counterpart to Ho Chi Minh City
wandrly_notes: Part of the 2016 Vietnam trip, older memories, but the Old Quarter and Halong Bay were the standouts.
destinations.tom_verdict: An older trip but Hanoi's Old Quarter and Halong Bay are worth it. Wander, eat, and get out to the bay.

destinations.name: Ho Chi Minh City
destinations.summary: Frenetic southern hub of markets, history and street food

activities.name: War Remnants Museum
activities.desc: A heavy but essential museum on the Vietnam War, told from the Vietnamese side

activities.name: Ben Thanh Market
activities.desc: The classic central market, haggle hard and graze the food stalls

activities.name: Cu Chi Tunnels day trip
activities.desc: The wartime tunnel network outside the city, a striking half-day trip

food_recommendations: Street pho and banh mi everywhere and dirt cheap
Grab a tiny plastic stool and eat where the locals do
nightlife: Bui Vien walking street is the backpacker nightlife strip
transport_tips: Crossing the road is an art, walk slowly and steadily and the scooters flow around you
Grab (bike or car) is cheap and the easiest way around
warnings: The traffic is genuinely overwhelming at first, thousands of scooters
Watch bags for snatch-theft in busy areas
similar_to: A busier, grittier southern counterpart to Hanoi
